records.ety: [OE. sethen, AS. se<?/an; akin to D. sieden, OHG. siodan, G. sieden, Icel. sj<?/<?/a, Sw. sjuda, Dan. syde, Goth. saubs a burnt offering. Cf. Sod, n., Sodden, Suds.]
records.morph: [imp. Seethed (?) (Sod (<?/), obs.); p. p. Seethed, Sodden (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Seething.]

defs: To decoct or prepare for food in hot liquid; to boil; as, to seethe flesh.
quotes: Set on the great pot, and seethe pottage for the sons of the prophets.

defs: To be a state of ebullition or violent commotion; to be hot; to boil.
quotes: A long Pointe, round which the Mississippi used to whirl, and seethe, and foam.
